items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment documentation is available
Previous inspection reports are checked
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Equipment is operated by a qualified operator
Warning notice, cautions or restrictions for safe operation and maintenance are clearly displayed and legible
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Manufacturer name, address, serial number and model are clearly marked or tagged
Safe working load and number of persons are clearly marked on the work platform
Maximum platform height and reach are clearly marked
Maximum travel height and travel directions are clearly marked
Power rating are provided and marked (for AC and/or DC)
Platform and guardrails electrical insulation data are provided
Platform surfaces are at least 18" (width & length) and are slip-resistant
Platform guardrails are provided and secure
Platform guardrails are 42" height (+/- 3")
Platform toeboard is fitted and at least 4" high
Moving parts are guarded
Personnel on the platform is protected against the hazards of moving parts of the aerial platforms.
Access to the platform is provided
Access gates or openings shall be properly closed per the manufacturer’s instructions
Electrical cables, wiring and components are undamaged
Safety harness anchorage is fitted in the platform
Battery is secure, guarded and ventilated
Outrigger position microswitches and interlocks are fully functional at their control panel
Air, Hydraulic and fuel systems are not leaking
Emergency stop button is available at ground control station
There are no loose nuts, bolts, missing, worn or damaged parts around the structure
The equipment is free of defective and/or corroded members/joints
Working environment is hazard free and machine can be safely operated
An hour meter is fitted
A fire extinguisher is fitted (type depends on operating location)
All moving parts are lubricated
All fluid levels for oil, water and hydraulic are adequate
Platform controls operate the machine correctly
Platform controls are clearly marked with direction and mode of operation
Platform controls accessible to the operator
Platform controls return to "off" or "neutral" position when released
Platform controls are protected against inadvertent operation
Over-ride control for power functions is fitted at ground level control panel
Lower controls return to "off" or "neutral" position when released
Lower controls are clearly marked for direction and mode of operation
Lower controls do not include drive and steering functions
Lower controls are protected against inadvertent operation
Boom raise/extend is operable from platform and ground control
Boom lower/retract is operable from platform and ground control
Platform rotation is operable from both platform and ground controls
Emergency stop button is operable from both platform and ground controls
Auxiliary/emergency lowering is operable from platform and ground controls
Auxiliary/emergency retracting is operable from platform and ground controls
Auxiliary/emergency rotating is operable from platform and ground controls
Hydraulic/Pneumatic systems are leak-free with platform raised
Tilt alarm is operable (5 degrees out of level) (manually depress the edge of the tilt switch to cause alarm condition)
Aerial platforms is fitted with a warning device at the platform which activates automatically when the aerial platform is at and beyond the slope
Tilt alarm is not combined with boom raise cut-out (dependent on machine manufacturer)
Steering is properly working when moving forward and in reverse directions
Brakes are in good working condition and can hold the machine on any slope (parking and moving)
Extendable axles function is satisfactory (where fitted)
Outriggers (stabilizers) extension-retraction operation is satisfactory
Extendable elevating assembly operation is satisfactory (retracted vs. extended)
Elevating assembly operation is satisfactory (elevated vs. lowered)
Wheels and tires are serviceable and undamaged
Travel alarm is operable
Platform lowering alarm is operable
Auxiliary power source available (to use in event of primary power loss)
Platform does not descend when in raised position and the power is off
Machine is safely drivable from the platform when raised (fast, slow, forward and reverse modes)
The platform does not descend when in raised position and the power is off
Engine powered machines fuel lines are supported to minimize chafing
Engine powered machines fuel lines are positioned to minimize exposure to engine and exhaust heat
Engine powered machines exhaust system is provided with muffler

Installation of Anti- Entrapment device on work platform REMARKS:This check list will be applied for Vehicle Mounted Elevating Work Platform - Inspected equipment with refrence to ANSI/SAIA A92.5 shall be equipped with DOCUMENTS: 1- ANSI/SAIA A92.5 - 2014 Issue: Boom - Supported Elevating Work Platforms 2- SAES-B067 - 2020 Issue: Safety Identification and Safety Colors 3- SA GI 7.025 -2018 Issue: Heavy Equipment Operator and Rigger Testing and
items.referenceStandard: Letter CIU-L- 019/2016 - ANSI/SAIA A92.2. Anti- Entrapment device on work platform. Certification
